Calculation.Text: /* =================================================================== Function: JSON_Map ( data ; calculationString ) Purpose: Provide a simple map function for JSON Params: data - the JSON legal text to feed the function calculationString - the operation to run durring each loop of the JSON Object or Array. (single quotes are converted to double quotes so escaping quotes is simpler) String Args: @value = This is the value that is currently in focus of the loop @index = The 0 index number used to traverse the loop. @key = The key used to get the value from data. For arrays it is same as @index for object it is the current key. @data = The data that is the first paramter of the array. (Used rarely but provides reference to orginal data set) Returns: Returns a modified JSON Object or Array. Requires: FileMaker v.18 or later. --------------------------------------------------------------------- Version: 1.2 Created: May 13, 2019 Author: Jared Hague Docs: https://blog.beezwax.net/lets-abstract-a-while-in-filemaker/ Download sample file =================================================================== */ While ( [ //Initial Variables ~result = ""; ~vid = Get ( UUIDNumber ); //variable id $fData[~vid] = JSONFormatElements ( data ); ~dataIndicator = Left ( $fData[~vid] ; 1 ) ; ~isValidData = Case ( ~dataIndicator = "[" ; 1 ; ~dataIndicator = "{" ; 2 ; 0 ); ~dataKeys = JSONListKeys ( $fData[~vid] ; "" ); ~dataLength = ValueCount ( ~dataKeys ); ~index = 0; ~resultIndex = 0; ~index = Case ( ~isValidData ; ~index ; ~dataLength + 1 ) //last chance to bail out ] ; //Condition ~index < ~dataLength ; [ //Logic ~key = GetValue ( ~dataKeys ; ~index + 1 ); ~value = Case ( ~isValidData = 1 ; JSONGetElement ( $fData[~vid] ; "[" & ~key & "]" ); ~isValidData = 2 ; JSONGetElement ( $fData[~vid] ; ~key ) ); ~item = Case ( ~isValidData = 1 ; Case ( Left ( ~value ; 1 ) = "[" ; ~value ; Left ( ~value ; 1 ) = "{" ; ~value ; Quote ( ~value ) ); ~isValidData = 2 ; Quote ( ~key ) & ":" & Quote ( ~value ) ); //this is the boolean test to see if the element is included in new array ~operation = Evaluate ( Substitute ( calculationString ; ["\'" ; "#@^"]; ["'" ; "\""]; ["#@^" ; "'"]; ["@value" ; Quote( ~value )]; ["@key" ; Quote( ~key )]; ["@index" ; ~index ]; ["@data";"$fData[" & ~vid & "]"] ) ); ~result = Case ( ~operation = "?" ; "Error" ; Case ( ~index = 0 ; ~operation ; ~result & ", " & ~operation ) ); ~index = ~index + 1 ] ; //Result Case ( ~isValidData ; Case ( ~result = "Error" ; "Error in operationString" ; Not IsEmpty ( ~result ) ; Case ( ~isValidData = 1 ; "[" & ~result & "]" ; ~isValidData = 2 ; "{" & ~result & "}" ) ) ; "Error in array data" ) )
